This Part applies to the Society and managing agents separately.

content_markdown:
1

###  Application and Definitions

1.1

Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:

  1. (1) a _UK Solvency II firm_ ;
  2. (2) in accordance with [Insurance General Application 3](/pra-rules/insurance-general-application#2e5a9cd32e6d4026895a5d543b990108 "Insurance General Application 3"), the _Society_ , as modified by [8](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#ecc3d891b4814943b25c2ad26a54b4ff "8");
  3. (3) in accordance with [Insurance General Application 3](/pra-rules/insurance-general-application#2e5a9cd32e6d4026895a5d543b990108 "Insurance General Application 3"), a _managing agent_ , as modified by [8](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#ecc3d891b4814943b25c2ad26a54b4ff "8");
  4. (4) a _third country branch undertaking_ (other than a _Swiss general insurer_);
  5. (5) a _UK ISPV_ ; and
  6. (6) a _third country insurance services provider_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

1.2

In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

_commencement date_

has the meaning given in [Insurance - Senior Managers Regime – Transitional Provisions](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---transitional-provisions "Senior Insurance Managers Regime – Transitional Provisions").

_current approved person approval_

means

     1. (1) an approval granted to a _person_ under section 59 of _FSMA_ (Approval for particular arrangements):
        1. (a) by the _PRA_ for the performance of a _PRA senior management function_ ; or
        2. (b) by the _FCA_ for the performance of an _FCA-designated senior management function_ or a _significant influence function_ ;
     2. but excludes a notice given under _section 59ZZA_ of _FSMA_ treating a _person_ as approved;
     3. (2) a deemed approval given by:
        1. (a) the _PRA_ following the submission of a _conversion notification_ ; or
        2. (b) the FCA under any equivalent process contained in rules in the _FCA Handbook_ ; or
     4. (3) for the purposes of [2.5](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#3c2dbf2827814aec8b08bb6f0d34bcf9 "2.5")(2) and [2.6](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#fe0f306fc2a04778914ea31775a4d4df "2.6")(1), an approval granted to that _person_ under section 59 of _FSMA_ by either the _PRA_ or the __FCA_ _to perform a _controlled function_ prior to the _commencement date_ but which ceased (i) on or before the _commencement date_ ; and (ii) during the six-_month_ period specified in [2.5](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#3c2dbf2827814aec8b08bb6f0d34bcf9 "2.5")(2) and [2.6](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#fe0f306fc2a04778914ea31775a4d4df "2.6")(1), as the case may be.

_regulatory body_

means any authority, body or _person_ having, or who has had, responsibility for the supervision or regulation of any _regulated activities_ or other financial services, whether within the _United Kingdom_ or overseas.

_significant influence function_

has the meaning given in the _FCA Handbook_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

1.3

In this Part, _PRA approved person_ also includes a _person_ in relation to whom a notice under _section 59ZZA_ has been given to an _authorised person_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

2

###  Application to Perform a PRA Senior Management Function

2.1

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must make a _PRA senior management approval application_ in accordance with [2.2](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#3c2dbf2827814aec8b08bb6f0d34bcf9 "2.2"), before the activities requiring approval commence.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.2

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must use Form A (long form) for a _PRA senior management approval application_ unless:

  1. (1) the _firm_ must use Form E under [2.3](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#b8a59bb95bee4c9d94a4901083300bc3 "2.3"); or
  2. (2) the _firm_ must use Form A (shortened form) under [2.6](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#fe0f306fc2a04778914ea31775a4d4df "2.6").

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.3

The _PRA_ directs that, subject to [2.4](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#88d8bfcb4a6c445c891aefd7b4acf6cf "2.4") and [2.5](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#ccf7d6f438e04610965d37fce4981a6c "2.5"), a _firm_ must use Form E for a _PRA senior management approval application_ if it is being made in respect of a _person_ who has a _current approved person approval_ and is either:

  1. (1) ceasing to perform a _PRA senior management function_ and taking up a new _PRA senior management function _for the same _firm_ or another member of its _group_ ; or
  2. (2) ceasing to perform an _FCA-designated senior management function_ or a _significant influence function_ and taking up a _PRA senior management function _for the _firm_ or another member of its _group_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.4

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must not use Form E for a _PRA senior management approval application_ if:

  1. (A1) a notification has been or should be made to the _PRA_ or _FCA_ under any of the following:
     1. (a) section 63(2A) of _FSMA_ (Duty to notify regulator of grounds for withdrawal of approval);
     2. (b) section 64C of _FSMA_ (Requirement for authorised person to notify regulator of disciplinary action);
  2. (1) a notification has been made or should be made to the _PRA_ under any of the rules in [Notifications 11](/pra-rules/notifications#24ae7653b5b24fe292939e4406080222 "Notifications 11") or to the _FCA_ under any equivalent provisions of the _FCA Handbook_ ;
  3. (2) any of the circumstances in [4.2](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#80b76b2e1bcb4ef49da92f5b7dc35c9f "4.2")(2)(a)-(d) apply in relation to:
     1. (a) any _controlled function_ that that _person_ is ceasing to perform; or
     2. (b) any _controlled function_ that that _person_ is continuing to perform in relation to that _firm_ or a _firm_ in the same _group_.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.5

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must not use Form E if the _person_ to whom the _PRA senior management approval application_ relates:

  1. (1) has never before been approved:
     1. (a) by the _PRA_ to perform a _controlled function_ ; or
     2. (b) by the _FCA_ to perform an _FCA-designated senior management function_ or a _significant influence function_
  2. for any _firm_ ;
  3. or
  4. (2) has not been the subject of a _current approved person approval_ in relation to any _firm_ for more than six _months_ prior to the date of application.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.6

  1. (1) The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must use Form A (shortened form) for a _PRA senior management approval application_ if:
     1. (a) the _person_ to whom the application relates:
        1. (i) has at the time of application a _current approved person approval_ ; or
        2. (ii) has had a _current approved person approval_ within the previous six _months_ ; and
     2. (b) there have been no matters arising in relation to the fitness and propriety of the _person_ to whom the _PRA senior management approval application_ relates which mean that the information provided to the _FCA_ or _PRA_ regarding fitness and propriety in connection with a _current approved person approval_ may have changed since the application for the _current approved person approval_ was made.
  2. (2) The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must not use Form A (shortened form) if the circumstances set out in [2.3](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#b8a59bb95bee4c9d94a4901083300bc3 "2.3") apply.

*Effective: 11/05/2023*

2.7

The _PRA_ directs that the form submitted for a _PRA senior management approval application_ must be accompanied by a _statement of responsibilities form_ or, where a _statement of responsibilities form_ has already been provided under [Key Function Holder – Notifications 2.2](/pra-rules/key-function-holder---notifications#ae5560f778244dd690fda57d3bc3398a "Key Function Holder – Notifications 2.2"), an updated version of it.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

2.8

[Deleted.]

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

2A

###  Section 59ZZA Application

2A.1

  1. (1) In the case of a _section 59ZZA application_ , the following directions shall have effect in substitution for any directions relating to the provision of information, documents, _statement of responsibilities_ and form of application which would otherwise apply on the making of an application under section 60 of _FSMA_.
  2. (2) The _PRA_ directs that the application must contain the information and be accompanied by such documents as are set out in the form approved by the _PRA_ for the purposes of this direction; except that where the application is in respect of a _person_ who holds a _current approved persons approval_ , Form E may be used in accordance with [2.3](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#b8a59bb95bee4c9d94a4901083300bc3 "2.3") instead.
  3. (3) The _PRA_ directs that the application must be accompanied by a _statement of responsibilities_ in accordance with [Insurance - Allocation of Responsibilities 5.4](/pra-rules/insurance---allocation-of-responsibilities#c5214fe954ee40399cabf80d53e721f0 "Insurance - Allocation of Responsibilities 5.4"), containing such information as is set out in the form approved by the _PRA_ for the purposes of this direction; except that where a Form E is used pursuant to (2) above, the application must provide a _statement of responsibilities_ specified in [2.7](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#fde9950bb7fe4119aae0f697bc247289 "2.7").
  4. (4) A _function_ performed by a _person_ in relation to whom a notice under _section 59ZZA_ of _FSMA_ could be given, shall not (otherwise than for the purposes of making an application under section 60 of _FSMA_), be treated as a _controlled function_ until the earliest of:
     1. (a) 12 weeks beginning on the day on which _IP completion day_ occurs;
     2. (b) the giving of the notice under _Section 59ZZA_ ; or
     3. (c) the notification by the _PRA_ of its decision to grant or refuse the application.
  5. (5) In this Chapter _statement of responsibilities form_ means for a _firm_ making a _section 59ZZA application_ the form to be completed by a _firm_ containing:
     1. (a) the information referred to in [Insurance – Allocation of Responsibilities 5.1](/pra-rules/insurance---allocation-of-responsibilities#2460a2c78b2c4be9a48bfb3ef8dbc120 "Insurance – Allocation of Responsibilities 5.1")(3);
     2. (b) in respect of 2A.1(3), the information required by section 60(2A) of _FSMA_ ; and
     3. (c) in respect of [Insurance – Allocation of Responsibilities 5.5](/pra-rules/insurance---allocation-of-responsibilities#734420181cb64230a36e2d80be43d5e6 "Insurance – Allocation of Responsibilities 5.5"), the information required by section 60(2A) of _FSMA_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

2B

###  SRO Firms

2B.1

[2A.1](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#0856162495e948dd8c114e38b2b3d817 "2A.1") shall apply to a _SRO firm_ as if:

  1. (1) the reference in [2A.1](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#0856162495e948dd8c114e38b2b3d817 "2A.1")(2) and (3) to the forms approved by the _PRA_ were references to the forms approved for the purposes of an application made by a _SRO firm_ ; and
  2. (2) the reference in [2A.1](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#0856162495e948dd8c114e38b2b3d817 "2A.1")(4)(a) to 12 weeks beginning on the day on which _IP completion day_ occurs were a reference to 12 weeks beginning on the day the _firm_ became a _SRO firm_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

3

###  Withdrawal of a PRA Senior Management Approval Application

3.1

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ withdrawing an outstanding _PRA senior management approval application_ must do so using Form B.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

4

###  Ceasing to Perform a PRA Senior Management Function

4.1

A _firm_ must notify the _PRA_ no later than seven _business days_ after a _person_ permanently ceases to perform a _PRA senior management function_ , using:

  1. (1) Form E pursuant to the direction in [2.3](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#b8a59bb95bee4c9d94a4901083300bc3 "2.3") if a _person_ permanently ceases to perform a _PRA senior management function_ and the _firm_ is also making an application for the same _person_ to perform a new _PRA senior management function_ ; and
  2. (2) in all other cases, Form C.

*Effective: 11/05/2023*

4.2

  1. (1) A _firm_ must notify the _PRA_ as soon as practicable after it becomes aware, or has information which reasonably suggests, that it will submit a qualified Form C in respect of a _PRA senior management function holder_.
  2. (2) Form C is qualified if the information it contains:
     1. (a) relates to the fact that the _firm_ has dismissed or suspended the _PRA senior management function holder _from its employment;
     2. (b) relates to the resignation by the _PRA senior management function holder _while under investigation by the _firm_ , the _PRA_ or any other _regulatory body_ ;
     3. (c) includes a notification under any of the provisions set out in [2.4](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#88d8bfcb4a6c445c891aefd7b4acf6cf "2.4")(A1) or (1); or
     4. (d) otherwise reasonably suggests that it may affect the _PRA’s_ assessment of the _PRA senior management function holder’s _fitness and propriety.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

4A

###  Application to Vary a Conditional Approval

4A.1

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ making an application to the _PRA_ under section 63ZA of _FSMA_ (for the variation of a conditional approval) must do so by submitting:

  1. (1) Form I; and
  2. (2) a _statement of responsibilities form_ for the _PRA approved person_ concerned.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

4A.2

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ withdrawing an application made under section 63ZA of _FSMA_ (Variation of senior manager’s approval at request of relevant authorised person) must do so using Form B.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5

###  Change in Details Relating to PRA Senior Management Function Holders

5.1

If a _PRA senior management function holder's _title, name or national insurance number changes, the _firm_ for which the _person_ performs a _PRA senior management function_ must notify the _PRA_ of that change within seven _business days_ of the _firm_ becoming aware of the matter, using Form D.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5.1A

If a _PRA senior management function holder_ has been temporarily absent for more than a 12 week period, the _firm_ for which the _person_ performs a _PRA senior management function_ must notify the _PRA_ of:

  1. (1) that absence within seven _business days_ of the end of the 12 week period; and
  2. (2) the _PRA senior management function holder’s_ return from absence within seven _business days_ of the date of their return,

in each case, using Form D.

*Effective: 02/06/2021*

5.2

If, in relation to a _firm_ which has submitted an application on either Form A (long or shortened form) or Form E, as required by [2.2](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#3c2dbf2827814aec8b08bb6f0d34bcf9 "2.2"), any of the details relating to _PRA senior management functions _are to change, the _firm_ must notify the _PRA_ using Form D as soon as reasonably practicable after the _firm_ becomes aware of the proposed change.

*Effective: 10/12/2018*

5.3

The _PRA_ directs that if a _PRA senior management function holder_ ceases to perform a _PRA senior management function_ for a _firm_ but continues to perform one or more _PRA senior management functions_ for the same _firm_ , the _firm_ must submit a revised _statement of responsibilities form_ for the remaining _PRA senior management function(s)_ , along with Form J.

*Effective: 11/05/2023*

6

###  Procedure for Making Applications and Notifications

6.1

The _PRA_ directs that save as required by [6.1A](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#46f2016363514b98ae129d6287607c79 "6.1A") or [6.2](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#c4e6d5afde3248c2b263149945addba1 "6.2"), a _firm_ must make any applications, notifications or submissions required by this Part by submitting the form specified using the _ONA system_.

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

6.1A

The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ making a _section 59ZZA application_ must make that application by submitting the information, documents, _statement of responsibilities_ and forms required by [2A](/pra-rules/insurance---senior-managers-regime---applications-and-notifications#29bc965a7abf4c50bc717f64cfaa2c90 "2A") in the manner set out in [Notifications 7](/pra-rules/notifications#8e186d20f3cf4d218117e0780f104f7c "Notifications 7").

*Effective: 31/12/2020*

6.2

If, under any direction or rule in this Part:

  1. (1) a _firm_ is required to make an application, notification or submission online; and
  2. (2) the information technology systems used by the _PRA_ fail and online submission is unavailable for 24 hours or more,

until such time as facilities for online submission are restored, a _firm_ must submit the specified form in the way set out in [Notifications 7](/pra-rules/notifications#8e186d20f3cf4d218117e0780f104f7c "Notifications 7").

*Effective: 07/03/2016*

6.3

  1. (1) The _PRA_ directs that, if a _firm_ is required to submit a revised _statement of responsibilities_ under section 62A of _FSMA_ , it must do so by submitting Form J with the revised _statement of responsibilities form_.
  2. (2) The _PRA_ directs that a _firm_ must not use Form J where the revisions are to be made as part of arrangements involving an application:
     1. (a) for approval for the _person_ performing the _PRA senior management function_ concerned to perform another _PRA senior management function_ or _relevant senior management function_ for the same _firm_ ; or
     2. (b) to vary under section 63ZA of _FSMA_ an approval for the _person_ performing the _PRA senior management function_ concerned to perform another _PRA senior management function_ or _relevant senior management function_ for the same _firm_.

*Effective: 11/05/2023*

7

###  Forms

7.1

  1. (1) Form A (long form) is available on the _PRA's_ website.
  2. (2) Form A (shortened form) is available on the _PRA's_ website.
  3. (3) Form B is available on the _PRA's_ website.
  4. (4) Form C may be found [here](https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/-/media/boe/files/prudential-regulation/authorisations/senior-managers-and-senior-insurance-managers-regimes-approvals/form-c-dec-2023.pdf "Form C").
  5. (5) Form D may be found [here](https://www.bankofengland.co.uk/-/media/boe/files/prudential-regulation/authorisations/senior-managers-and-senior-insurance-managers-regimes-approvals/form-d-dec-2023.pdf "Form D").
  6. (6) Form E is available on the _PRA's_ website.
  7. (6A) Form I is available on the _PRA's_ website.
  8. (6B) Form J is available on the _PRA's_ website.
  9. (7) The _statement of responsibilities form_ is available on the _PRA's_ website.

*Effective: 21/12/2023*

8

###  Lloyd’s

8.1

This Part applies to the _Society_ and _managing agents_ separately.

*Effective: 07/03/2016*
content_html:
1

Application and Definitions

1.1

Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to:

  1. (1) a UK Solvency II firm;
  2. (2) in accordance with Insurance General Application 3, the Society, as modified by 8;
  3. (3) in accordance with Insurance General Application 3, a managing agent, as modified by 8;
  4. (4) a third country branch undertaking (other than a Swiss general insurer);
  5. (5) a UK ISPV; and
  6. (6) a third country insurance services provider.
  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 1.1 before 31/12/2020

1.2

In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

commencement date

has the meaning given in Insurance - Senior Managers Regime – Transitional Provisions.

current approved person approval

means

    1. (1) an approval granted to a person under section 59 of FSMA (Approval for particular arrangements):
      1. (a) by the PRA for the performance of a PRA senior management function; or
      2. (b) by the FCA for the performance of an FCA-designated senior management function or a significant influence function;
    2. but excludes a notice given under section 59ZZA of FSMA treating a person as approved;
    3. (2) a deemed approval given by:
      1. (a) the PRA following the submission of a conversion notification; or
      2. (b) the FCA under any equivalent process contained in rules in the FCA Handbook; or
    4. (3) for the purposes of 2.5(2) and 2.6(1), an approval granted to that person under section 59 of FSMA by either the PRA or the FCA to perform a controlled function prior to the commencement date but which ceased (i) on or before the commencement date; and (ii) during the six-month period specified in 2.5(2) and 2.6(1), as the case may be.

regulatory body

means any authority, body or person having, or who has had, responsibility for the supervision or regulation of any regulated activities or other financial services, whether within the United Kingdom or overseas.

significant influence function

has the meaning given in the FCA Handbook.

  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 1.2 before 31/12/2020

1.3

In this Part, PRA approved person also includes a person in relation to whom a notice under section 59ZZA has been given to an authorised person.

  • 31/12/2020

2

Application to Perform a PRA Senior Management Function

2.1

The PRA directs that a firm must make a PRA senior management approval application in accordance with 2.2, before the activities requiring approval commence.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.1 before 10/12/2018

2.2

The PRA directs that a firm must use Form A (long form) for a PRA senior management approval application unless:

  1. (1) the firm must use Form E under 2.3; or
  2. (2) the firm must use Form A (shortened form) under 2.6.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.2 before 10/12/2018

2.3

The PRA directs that, subject to 2.4 and 2.5, a firm must use Form E for a PRA senior management approval application if it is being made in respect of a person who has a current approved person approval and is either:

  1. (1) ceasing to perform a PRA senior management function and taking up a new PRA senior management function for the same firm or another member of its group; or
  2. (2) ceasing to perform an FCA-designated senior management function or a significant influence function and taking up a PRA senior management function for the firm or another member of its group.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.3 before 10/12/2018

2.4

The PRA directs that a firm must not use Form E for a PRA senior management approval application if:

  1. (A1) a notification has been or should be made to the PRA or FCA under any of the following:
    1. (a) section 63(2A) of FSMA (Duty to notify regulator of grounds for withdrawal of approval);
    2. (b) section 64C of FSMA (Requirement for authorised person to notify regulator of disciplinary action);
  2. (1) a notification has been made or should be made to the PRA under any of the rules in Notifications 11 or to the FCA under any equivalent provisions of the FCA Handbook;
  3. (2) any of the circumstances in 4.2(2)(a)-(d) apply in relation to:
    1. (a) any controlled function that that person is ceasing to perform; or
    2. (b) any controlled function that that person is continuing to perform in relation to that firm or a firm in the same group.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.4 before 10/12/2018

2.5

The PRA directs that a firm must not use Form E if the person to whom the PRA senior management approval application relates:

  1. (1) has never before been approved:
    1. (a) by the PRA to perform a controlled function; or
    2. (b) by the FCA to perform an FCA-designated senior management function or a significant influence function
  2. for any firm;
  3. or
  4. (2) has not been the subject of a current approved person approval in relation to any firm for more than six months prior to the date of application.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.5 before 10/12/2018

2.6

  1. (1) The PRA directs that a firm must use Form A (shortened form) for a PRA senior management approval application if:
    1. (a) the person to whom the application relates:
      1. (i) has at the time of application a current approved person approval; or
      2. (ii) has had a current approved person approval within the previous six months; and
    2. (b) there have been no matters arising in relation to the fitness and propriety of the person to whom the PRA senior management approval application relates which mean that the information provided to the FCA or PRA regarding fitness and propriety in connection with a current approved person approval may have changed since the application for the current approved person approval was made.
  2. (2) The PRA directs that a firm must not use Form A (shortened form) if the circumstances set out in 2.3 apply.
  • 11/05/2023
  • Past version of 2.6 before 11/05/2023

2.7

The PRA directs that the form submitted for a PRA senior management approval application must be accompanied by a statement of responsibilities form or, where a statement of responsibilities form has already been provided under Key Function Holder – Notifications 2.2, an updated version of it.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 2.7 before 10/12/2018

2.8

[Deleted.]

  • 07/03/2016

2A

Section 59ZZA Application

2A.1

  1. (1) In the case of a section 59ZZA application, the following directions shall have effect in substitution for any directions relating to the provision of information, documents, statement of responsibilities and form of application which would otherwise apply on the making of an application under section 60 of FSMA.
  2. (2) The PRA directs that the application must contain the information and be accompanied by such documents as are set out in the form approved by the PRA for the purposes of this direction; except that where the application is in respect of a person who holds a current approved persons approval, Form E may be used in accordance with 2.3 instead.
  3. (3) The PRA directs that the application must be accompanied by a statement of responsibilities in accordance with Insurance - Allocation of Responsibilities 5.4, containing such information as is set out in the form approved by the PRA for the purposes of this direction; except that where a Form E is used pursuant to (2) above, the application must provide a statement of responsibilities specified in 2.7.
  4. (4) A function performed by a person in relation to whom a notice under section 59ZZA of FSMA could be given, shall not (otherwise than for the purposes of making an application under section 60 of FSMA), be treated as a controlled function until the earliest of:
    1. (a) 12 weeks beginning on the day on which IP completion day occurs;
    2. (b) the giving of the notice under Section 59ZZA; or
    3. (c) the notification by the PRA of its decision to grant or refuse the application.
  5. (5) In this Chapter statement of responsibilities form means for a firm making a section 59ZZA application the form to be completed by a firm containing:
    1. (a) the information referred to in Insurance – Allocation of Responsibilities 5.1(3);
    2. (b) in respect of 2A.1(3), the information required by section 60(2A) of FSMA; and
    3. (c) in respect of Insurance – Allocation of Responsibilities 5.5, the information required by section 60(2A) of FSMA.
  • 31/12/2020

2B

SRO Firms

2B.1

2A.1 shall apply to a SRO firm as if:

  1. (1) the reference in 2A.1(2) and (3) to the forms approved by the PRA were references to the forms approved for the purposes of an application made by a SRO firm; and
  2. (2) the reference in 2A.1(4)(a) to 12 weeks beginning on the day on which IP completion day occurs were a reference to 12 weeks beginning on the day the firm became a SRO firm.
  • 31/12/2020

3

Withdrawal of a PRA Senior Management Approval Application

3.1

The PRA directs that a firm withdrawing an outstanding PRA senior management approval application must do so using Form B.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 3.1 before 10/12/2018

4

Ceasing to Perform a PRA Senior Management Function

4.1

A firm must notify the PRA no later than seven business days after a person permanently ceases to perform a PRA senior management function, using:

  1. (1) Form E pursuant to the direction in 2.3 if a person permanently ceases to perform a PRA senior management function and the firm is also making an application for the same person to perform a new PRA senior management function; and
  2. (2) in all other cases, Form C.
  • 11/05/2023
  • Past version of 4.1 before 11/05/2023

4.2

  1. (1) A firm must notify the PRA as soon as practicable after it becomes aware, or has information which reasonably suggests, that it will submit a qualified Form C in respect of a PRA senior management function holder.
  2. (2) Form C is qualified if the information it contains:
    1. (a) relates to the fact that the firm has dismissed or suspended the PRA senior management function holder from its employment;
    2. (b) relates to the resignation by the PRA senior management function holder while under investigation by the firm, the PRA or any other regulatory body;
    3. (c) includes a notification under any of the provisions set out in 2.4(A1) or (1); or
    4. (d) otherwise reasonably suggests that it may affect the PRA’s assessment of the PRA senior management function holder’s fitness and propriety.
  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 4.2 before 10/12/2018

4A

Application to Vary a Conditional Approval

4A.1

The PRA directs that a firm making an application to the PRA under section 63ZA of FSMA (for the variation of a conditional approval) must do so by submitting:

  1. (1) Form I; and
  2. (2) a statement of responsibilities form for the PRA approved person concerned.
  • 10/12/2018

4A.2

The PRA directs that a firm withdrawing an application made under section 63ZA of FSMA (Variation of senior manager’s approval at request of relevant authorised person) must do so using Form B.

  • 10/12/2018

5

Change in Details Relating to PRA Senior Management Function Holders

5.1

If a PRA senior management function holder's title, name or national insurance number changes, the firm for which the person performs a PRA senior management function must notify the PRA of that change within seven business days of the firm becoming aware of the matter, using Form D.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 5.1 before 10/12/2018

5.1A

If a PRA senior management function holder has been temporarily absent for more than a 12 week period, the firm for which the person performs a PRA senior management function must notify the PRA of:

  1. (1) that absence within seven business days of the end of the 12 week period; and
  2. (2) the PRA senior management function holder’s return from absence within seven business days of the date of their return,

in each case, using Form D.

  • 02/06/2021

5.2

If, in relation to a firm which has submitted an application on either Form A (long or shortened form) or Form E, as required by 2.2, any of the details relating to PRA senior management functions are to change, the firm must notify the PRA using Form D as soon as reasonably practicable after the firm becomes aware of the proposed change.

  • 10/12/2018
  • Past version of 5.2 before 10/12/2018

5.3

The PRA directs that if a PRA senior management function holder ceases to perform a PRA senior management function for a firm but continues to perform one or more PRA senior management functions for the same firm, the firm must submit a revised statement of responsibilities form for the remaining PRA senior management function(s), along with Form J.

  • 11/05/2023
  • Past version of 5.3 before 11/05/2023

6

Procedure for Making Applications and Notifications

6.1

The PRA directs that save as required by 6.1A or 6.2, a firm must make any applications, notifications or submissions required by this Part by submitting the form specified using the ONA system.

  • 31/12/2020
  • Past version of 6.1 before 31/12/2020

6.1A

The PRA directs that a firm making a section 59ZZA application must make that application by submitting the information, documents, statement of responsibilities and forms required by 2A in the manner set out in Notifications 7.

  • 31/12/2020

6.2

If, under any direction or rule in this Part:

  1. (1) a firm is required to make an application, notification or submission online; and
  2. (2) the information technology systems used by the PRA fail and online submission is unavailable for 24 hours or more,

until such time as facilities for online submission are restored, a firm must submit the specified form in the way set out in Notifications 7.

  • 07/03/2016

6.3

  1. (1) The PRA directs that, if a firm is required to submit a revised statement of responsibilities under section 62A of FSMA, it must do so by submitting Form J with the revised statement of responsibilities form.
  2. (2) The PRA directs that a firm must not use Form J where the revisions are to be made as part of arrangements involving an application:
    1. (a) for approval for the person performing the PRA senior management function concerned to perform another PRA senior management function or relevant senior management function for the same firm; or
    2. (b) to vary under section 63ZA of FSMA an approval for the person performing the PRA senior management function concerned to perform another PRA senior management function or relevant senior management function for the same firm.
  • 11/05/2023
  • Past version of 6.3 before 11/05/2023

7

Forms

7.1

  1. (1) Form A (long form) is available on the PRA's website.
  2. (2) Form A (shortened form) is available on the PRA's website.
  3. (3) Form B is available on the PRA's website.
  4. (4) Form C may be found here.
  5. (5) Form D may be found here.
  6. (6) Form E is available on the PRA's website.
  7. (6A) Form I is available on the PRA's website.
  8. (6B) Form J is available on the PRA's website.
  9. (7) The statement of responsibilities form is available on the PRA's website.
  • 21/12/2023
  • Past version of 7.1 before 21/12/2023

8

Lloyd’s

8.1

This Part applies to the Society and managing agents separately.

  • 07/03/2016
